REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, PLAINTIFF TIFFANY SPURLOCK , AN INDIVIDUAL NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR 1) INTERLOCUTORY JUDGMENT OF PARTITION ON THE PLEADINGS AND 2) APPOINTMENT OF REFEREE IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. FURTHER, THE COURT IS NOT PERSUADED THAT THE ORDER REQUEST MAY BE OBTAINED ON A MOTION IN A MANNER OTHER THAN A MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/ADJUDICATION AS WAS THE CASE IN ALL ANALOGIES AUTHORITIES CITED BY THE MOVING PARTY. REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED, 10 DAYS TO ANSWER. CROSS-COMPLAINT SUFFICIENTLY STATES CAUSES OF ACTION FOR CONVERSION AND QUIET TITLE. REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IS DENIED AS IT IS MADE IN VIOLATION OF CRC 3.1113(L). (SEE PAGES 15-17 OF CROSS-DEFENDANT'S DOCUMENT TITLED "CROSS-DEFENDANT TIFFANY J. SPURLOCK'S NOTICE OF DEMURRER" TO THE EXTENT MOVING PARTY IS ATTEMPTING TO OBTAIN AN ORDER STRIKING PORTIONS OF DEFENDANT'S ANSWER, THE MOTION IS DENIED AS IT IS NOT PROPERLY NOTICED. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER THAT IS VERBATIM WITH THE COURT'S TENTATIVE RULING AND IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B) JUDGE: RONALD E. QUIDACHAY ; CLERK: L. YOUNG ; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

DEMURRER to CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-31-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

PLAINTIFF TIFFANY SPURLOCK , AN INDIVIDUAL DEMURRER to CROSS COMPLAINT
is OVERRULED, 10 days to answer. Cross-Complaint sufficiently states causes of action for conversion and quiet title. Request for Judicial notice is denied as it is made in violation of CRC 3.1113(l). (See pages 15-17 of Cross-Defendant's document titled "Cross-Defendant Tiffany J. Spurlock's Notice of Demurrer" To the extent moving party is attempting to obtain an order striking portions of defendant's answer, the motion is denied as it is not properly noticed.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the court's tentative ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b)
Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay ; Clerk: L. Young ; Not Reported. (D501)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For 1) Interlocutory Judgment Of Partition On The Pleadings And 2) Appointment Of Referee
Off Calendar Oct-17-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearance

Natalie Myung Esq., Talkov Law Group, 949-888-8800 via zoom for plaintiff
Dennis Vann Esq., Law Office of Dennis Vann, via zoom for defendant

Ms. Myung contests to the tentative. Ms. Myung makes an oral request for an appraisal which is not in the moving paper. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF TIFFANY SPURLOCK , AN INDIVIDUAL Notice Of Motion And Motion For 1) Interlocutory Judgment Of Partition On The Pleadings And 2) Appointment Of Referee is DENIED without prejudice. Moving party fails to address January 1, 2023 Partition of Real Property Act, CCP 974.311 et seq. Further, the Court is not persuaded that the order request may be obtained on a motion in a manner other than a Motion for Summary judgment/Adjudication as was the case in all analogies authorities cited by the moving party.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Vanessa Wu; Not Reported. (D501)
